title: viola uliginosa
intro: Viola uliginosa, also known as marsh violet, is a perennial plant species in the family Violaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various respiratory issues, such as bronchitis, coughs, and colds, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its health benefits, including antioxidant and antimicrobial effects, and may help alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia. The herbal preparations of Viola uliginosa include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
